Copy Accelerator: Unique Mechanism

  

In a Nutshell:

The unique mechanism explains why our prospect is struggling or suffering to achieve a goal…

 

And how they can overcome that problem.

 

At its core, the unique mechanism is always a two-part logical connection.

  

There are two parts to the unique mechanism:

 

1. Unique Mechanism Behind the Problem our Prospect Faces.

 

2. Unique Mechanism Behind the Solution to that Problem.

 

It’s important to identify the unique mechanism behind both the problem AND the solution.

 

By showing the unique mechanism behind the problem, you get the prospect to understand why they’ve failed in the past.

 

By showing the unique mechanism behind the solution, you get the prospect to believe that our product will bring them success where other alternatives have failed.



Put Another Way: 

Typically by the time our prospect comes to us, they’ve tried numerous solutions to their problem already. In the past, these other solutions may have not worked at all, worked partially, or worked fully for a period of time (before they stopped working).

 

Our job as a copywriter is to educate our prospect about the REAL reason why past solutions didn’t bring them permanent results. We do this by explaining that their knowledge about the problem is incomplete. They’ve been missing ONE CRUCIAL PIECE OF INFORMATION, and this “knowledge gap” is what’s been holding them back.

 

 

Typically the unique mechanism is presented in one of two different ways:

Problem -> Solution -> Avatar Created/Found Product That Matches Solution



Problem -> Product Category Type Is The Solution -> Specific Product Is THE Superior Type of Solution



CASE STUDY



The Background - From Mattia



Hi Stefan,

 

Talking to Justin he told me we could use the pre workout offer as an example of the mechanism for Tuesday CA call.

 

That would be incredibly useful because I’m working on it right now and trying to figuring out what mechanism would appeal the most to my audience (men who want to be bigger, stronger and leaner).

 

So I’d absolutely love to see process you use the find the right now.

 

I don’t know if you’d find it useful or not, but I attached you the formulation.

 

Let me know if you need anything else.

 

Thanks a lot,

Mattia

 

 









II. The Process



1. Pick ingredients to look into.

 

I picked Beta Alanine to start with.

 

Why?

 

Pretty simple: second highest dosage and I was less familiar with it than the other ingredients.

 

 

2. “Google” Beta Alanine



In this case, literally the first result is a gold mine.

https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/beta-alanine-101

 

 

 

3. Now pull out everything interesting from the article + highlight interesting things:  





The brain is like a large spider web that’s interconnected by pathways. 



Messages or "signals" are sent via these pathways from point to point. 



These "points" are what we call neurons. 



And these neurons basically act as the controller of how the information being sent flows from point to point. 



How? 



When one neuron receives enough signals from another neuron that’s connected to it via these pathways…



It causes the neuron to "fire" it's own signals to other neurons within the "spider web."



As a whole, this "spider web" of interconnected pathways have many different "teams of neurons…”



Sort of like “groups of points”



These “groups” work together as a collective which allows you to perform specific tasks throughout your day. 



Each "neuron team" then sends out their own "unique message" through these pathways within the "spider web" to other teams of neurons…





These messages are sent to different parts of the body from the brain to the spinal cord and nerves located throughout the body(the peripheral nervous system).



When this happens…



The person can perform a specific task in the real world. 



However, when you have drugs in the brain, it disrupts the flow of these signals that are being sent from neuron to neuron…



Causing you to do things you would not do if you were not high.



For example: Marijuana and Heroin are very similar to the natural neurotransmitters we have in the body…



But they do not activate in the same way. 



Therefore, this ends up with abnormal messages being sent in this "spider web" of neurotransmitters.



The scary thing is…



Drugs can alter very important parts of your brain where you use to stay alive. 







Now, there are three important areas of the brain that are being affected everytime you take part in your compulsive behavior.



The Basal Ganglia is the part of the brain where…



Motivations, pleasurable effects such as healthy eating, socializing, and sex... and the formation of habits and routines reside.



This section of the brain forms a key connection to what is sometimes known as "reward circuit" 



And this is important to know because drugs over-activate this part of the brain. 



And since it feels good... to get the high…



Your body is naturally going to crave more. 



Because in essence... it's in our nature to feel good.



But the problem is... with the drug use... 



The brain begins to adapt to the presence of the drug, desensitizing you...



Which means you need a bigger serving size to satisfy your craving.



And with each "hit" you take…



It becomes much more difficult to find pleasure in other things outside of the drug.



Now another part of the brain that is heavily involved in the addiction process…



The Extended Amygdala…



Is the part of the brain that activates once the "high" begins to fade.



This is the second step in the process.



Let me tell you how this part works.



When you feel anxiety, irritability, and unease…



This part of the brain flares up.



These symptoms usually come after you come down because that's when withdrawals begin to hit.



The longer you've used the drug, the harder this part of the brain activates, and the stronger withdrawal you feel.



Then if it gets to a point where the drug user has been using or doing for so long…



He would rather use the drug to relieve the pain from withdrawal, then to actually get high.



It then becomes a never-ending cycle of doom.



The final part of the process involves…



The prefrontal cortex.



This part of the brain gives you the power to think, plan, solve problems, make decisions, and exert self-control over impulses.



So when the user is compulsively using…



He is compulsive because he has shifted his powers from this part of the brain to the previous two parts of the brain.



That's why when a person who is addicted to something and wants to really stop…



But out of nowhere, they catch themselves with a needle in their arm or a beer in their hand...



It's because in one moment, the prefrontal cortex part is in charge... 



Hence: "I don't want to do this anymore"...



But then in the next moment, they are feeling stressed, uneasy, or irritable (Extended amygdala is in control)...



Causing the body to move in action towards the drug to get them relief...



Just to reach the (Basal Ganglia) part of the brain where pleasure resides.



So basically, when you feel shame in one moment because you’ve realized you lost control… your “thinking brain” is in control.



But when you find yourself with incredible urges to get your fix, the “irritable brain” is in control and will not stop until…



...the Pleasure Part of the brain wakes back up…



And the body gets back to ease… temporarily.









Another thing to note is…



Scientists believed that the chemical dopamine, known as the pleasure drug, was the cause of the addiction.



Afterall, dopamine is what makes us feel good.



However, new studies show that dopamine has more to do with getting us to repeat the use... moreso than actually causing us to "feel food."



A burst of dopamine is activated usually by activities that are pleasurable to the person.



Bu the activation... it tells the rest of the body that something important is happening and must be remembered. 



By this action,  it makes it easier to keep repeating the vice over and over without thinking about it.



And thus, the new self-destructing habit is formed... 



Causing the body to go into a downward spiral of destruction.



The large surges of the dopamine being released teach the brain to look for pleasurable activities that will reignite the surge of Dopamine so that the user can continue to feel this sense of pleasure. This connection to the drug becomes stronger with time and more use, making it easier and feel less "bad" to use it.



Why not do small pleasurable activities that are healthy in every day life?

Well because when drugs are in the mix...



It's just not that exciting to the brain anymore.



And because of that... the level of dopamine released is not enough.



Therefore, the user seeks the drug because the brain remembers what "really made him feel good" and goes back to that.



And as the tolerance begins to build, the vicious cycle becomes stronger with repetitive drug use...



Slowly falling into a dark hole that seemingly feels impossible to get out of.



That's why heavy drug users eventually feel apathetic, lifeless, demotivated, and unable to enjoy the things they used to enjoy.



Nothing brings them pleasure like a "nice hit" can.



 

 

SPG: So Histidine is already very interesting to me. I’d find out what it is right now, then see how it works.

 

Histidine = an amino acid used in the bio synthesis of proteins.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Histidine#targetText=Histidine%20(symbol%20His%20or%20H,in%20the%20biosynthesis%20of%20proteins.

 

 

SPG: Okay so now that I have this, I pretty much already have a rough idea for the unique mechanism behind the problem and solution:

 

Your muscles have a built-in “limiter” that controls how much mass you can gain, how fast muscles grow, how strong you become, etc.

 

It’s called Histidine and it’s an amino acid.

 

Histidine in general is good and plays lots of important roles in your body.

 

But when you’re working out, high levels of Histidine can limit something called carnosine. 



Carnosine is another amino acid that helps with improved athletic performance, better workouts, etc. 



So for the best results, you have to tell Histidine to STOP limiting the growth of your muscles and your strength, so you can produce more carnosine and get the best results. 







 

 

 

Now though let me keep reading…

 

Supplementing with beta-alanine has been shown to elevate carnosine levels in muscles by 80% (4, 5, 6, 7, 8).

This is how carnosine acts during exercise:

Glucose is broken down: Glycolysis is the breakdown of glucose, which is the main source of fuel during high-intensity exercise.

Lactate is produced: As you exercise, your muscles break glucose down into lactic acid. This is converted into lactate, which produces hydrogen ions (H+).

Muscles become more acidic: The hydrogen ions reduce the pH level in your muscles, making them more acidic.

Fatigue sets in: Muscle acidity blocks glucose breakdown and reduces your muscles' ability to contract. This causes fatigue (8, 9, 10).

Carnosine buffer: Carnosine serves as a buffer against the acid, reducing the acidity in muscles during high-intensity exercise (8, 11).

 

^^^

 

SPG: so this is pretty much already laid out for me, and wrapped together with a nice tidy bow. 



Reading this also makes me curious about the idea of “acidic muscles” or why, if you’re not getting the gym results you want, it could be that too much acid in your muscles is to blame. I’ll probably take this and then tease it in the lead.

 

 

So now I’d go back to this part:



In your muscles, histidine levels are normally high and beta-alanine levels low, which limits the production of carnosine (1Trusted Source, 4Trusted Source).

 

Now we know that for better workouts and more gains, we need a carnosine buffer. And we know that when Histidine Levels are high and carnosine levels are low, we don’t get that buffer.  

 





4. So We Can Restate Our Mechanism As This:



Mechanism of Problem: 



Your muscles have a built in limiter that stops you from working out longer, harder, and better. It also can sabotage your muscle growth too. 



It’s called Histidine. 



When Histidine levels are high, it stops your muscles from producing something called carnosine.



Why is carnosine important?



Normally when you work out, your muscles break glucose into something called lactic acid. The more acidic your muscles become, the less they can contract. This causes fatigue and limits effectiveness of workouts. 



But carnosine acts as a buffer against this acid. So when carnosine levels are higher, your muscles aren’t as fatigued, and you get better workouts. [STUDIES]



When Histidine Levels are high though, your muscles don’t produce as much carnosine. So your workouts aren’t as good. 



Mechanism of Solution: 



So the mechanism of the solution is to simply raise our carnosine levels. By doing this, our muscles are protected, we work out harder, become stronger, etc. 



[HERE’S WHERE YOU’D INCLUDE GENERAL PROOF ABOUT HIGHER CARNOSINE LEVELS = BETTER WORKOUTS]



And one of the best ways to increase carnosine levels is through beta-alanine.



[HERE’S WHERE YOU SHOW PROOF OF BETA ALANINE HELPING VIA MORE STUDIES]



Supplementing with beta-alanine has been shown to elevate carnosine levels in muscles by 80% (4, 5, 6, 7, 8).

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3374095/

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20479615

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/17690198

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4501114/

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/17690198

Increases Time to Exhaustion

Studies show that beta-alanine helps increase your time to exhaustion (TTE).

In other words, it helps you exercise for longer periods at a time. A study in cyclists found that four weeks of supplements increased total work completed by 13%, increasing an additional 3.2% after 10 weeks (3, 5, 8, 12).

Similarly, 20 men on a comparable cycling test increased their time to exhaustion by 13–14% after four weeks of beta-alanine supplements (13).

Benefits Shorter-Duration Exercises

In general, muscle acidosis limits the duration of high-intensity exercise.

For this reason, beta-alanine specifically helps performance during high-intensity and short-duration exercise lasting one to several minutes.

One study revealed that six weeks of taking beta-alanine increased TTE by 19% during high-intensity interval training (HIIT) (14).

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22973486/

In another study, 18 rowers who supplemented for seven weeks were 4.3 seconds faster than the placebo group in a 2,000-meter race lasting over 6 minutes (15).

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20671038/

 

Other Benefits

For older adults, beta-alanine can help increase muscle endurance (16).

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18992136/

In resistance training, it can boost training volume and reduce fatigue.

L-Carnitine Tartrate

And I’d say how the more your muscles are optimized (thanks to elevated carnosine levels), the more fat you can burn. So L-Carnitine simply helps transport fat to those muscles, where it gets burned for energy. And I’d talk about how in this case, you’re literally turning fat into muscle.

Alpha GPC

CASE STUDY



Working Problem Justin Is Facing



Product:  A course called “1000 buyers a day” - This will show people how to scale an offer from scratch to 1000 buyers a day.



Target buyer: business owners in our space, and copywriters



Problem:  



Their offer can't scale further because it's not converting well enough.



They dont really know what to test to make it convert better.



They can hire a copywriter to do it for them (but thats expensive and doesnt always work out).  



They  know they could try  new sales pages, new leads, new upsells, but they arent sure where to start.



They think a new traffic source might be  the solution (it’s not)



So they’re stuck at their current sales per day, and it’s probably waning. 



My course will  show them how  to fix their offer, so they can boost conversions, and AOV  -  and  then they  can scale to 1000+ sales a day.





Mechanism Ideas



I have two ideas for this….



 Some type of software where you enter simple data into  it (i.e. conversions on  sales page, upsell take rates, or total AOV) and then it tells you what you need to fix.



            I.e. if  you have decent conversions and your AOV sucks, we will target the upsells.



            Each one of these will have their own video showing you how to fix it.



 The  second option is I just put together the course how I would normally put it together - and it covers everything from offer, ads, copy, upsells, downsells etc…  



 My worry is that this is too much info -  and there’s not really a unique mechanism for it.  It’s more like here’s 8 videos of everything I know about scaling an offer.  



II. SPG Thoughts



So if it were me, I’d probably focus on some variant of #1. And the reason why I like this option is because it allows you to “pinpoint” the problem. 



Here are some things that I know people in our space love: 



1. Leverage/Levers

2. 80/20

3. Outsized Wins

4. Easy Wins

5. Simplicity 

6. AI/Algorithms/Tools



So if it were me I would probably take #1 and look at doing something like: 



Problem: Your offer can’t scale and you’re not sure why. You have ideas but you also don’t know what to test first, how to start testing it, and what the results will be. You worry that figuring out why your offer isn’t scaling is going to cost you tens-of-thousands of dollars (copywriters, traffic for testing, new products to test, etc). So you either feel paralyzed and don’t do anything, or you do spend tens-of-thousands of dollars but you’re not confident in your tests and it feels like you’re going around blind. Find an interesting term or phrase here. Marketing Quicksand?



Solution: 

One thing we know from the 80/20 Principle is that there’s always going to be a few specific things that bring you the most outsized results. And the truth is, when it comes to your funnel, really there’s always one single needle mover that will bring you by far the biggest bang for your buck. 



Plus what’s so amazing about our industry is that we don’t have to hypothesize here. There are a few categories of offers, and for each of those categories, all of the best offers inside of them end up having pretty much the same benchmarks or metrics.



For example: AOV, CPA, CPL, LTV, CNV, CTR, Engagement, etc. 



What this means is that you really just need to compare the stats you have now to the stats of the top performing offers in your category. By doing this you can see exactly where your offer is on par with the best, and where it’s falling short. 



Plus not only that, but you can see by how much your offer is falling short.



And then guess what…



Wherever your offer is deviating the most from industry controls, that’s where you focus your energy and efforts first.



And now it’s really easy to do that, and here’s why.



1. Me and my team have identified “8” Benchmarks/Metrics that are the most vital to the success of any offer. 



2. And we took these from “X” different niches: Health, Financial, Biz Opp, Self-Help, etc. 



3. Then we went to the offer owners who run the top controls in EACH of these niches, and got them to spill the beans on exactly what their data and metrics look like. These are ONLY offers that are doing at least 1,000 sales per day. 



4. Then, we built a simple and beautiful software program that allows you to compare your metrics to theirs. So you simply pick your category, fill in as many of the fields as you can, and then the software rates your benchmarks vs. each of the top controls in your space. 



5. Plus for each metric, the software shows you the exact percentage difference between your offer, and the control. Whether that be below or above. 



6. Wherever the biggest point of underperformance is, that’s where you focus first.



7. And of course I don’t leave you hanging. Instead, for each metric, I’ve created specific, high-level trainings for how to improve those and get them to control-level. 



8. So the result is you plug in your offer metric, find out the SINGLE place where you need to focus (plus know exactly what success looks like), and then you’re shown a step-by-step process to get there.
